title: Failed to parse Verilog code with escaped identifiers
body:
### Version

Yosys 0.33+53 (git sha1 e2b613355, clang 14.0.0-1ubuntu1.1 -fPIC -Os)

### On which OS did this happen?

Linux

### Reproduction Steps

Please consider this example:
```verilog
module esc (input \a[1] , output [2:0] o);
    wire a[1:0];
    assign o = {\a[1] , a[0], a[1]};
endmodule
```

Start yosys with this command:
```
yosys -p "read_verilog esc.v"
```

### Expected Behavior

Section 3.7.1 of Verilog Standard (1364-2005) defines the "escaped identifiers".

> _Escaped identifiers_ shall start with the backslash character (\\) and end with white space (space, tab, newline). They provide a means of including any of the printable ASCII characters in an identifier (the decimal values 33 through 126, or 21 through 7E in hexadecimal).
> Neither the leading backslash character nor the terminating white space is considered to be part of the identifier. Therefore, an escaped identifier `\cpu3` is treated the same as a nonescaped identifier `cpu3`.

Therefore, this module declaration seems valid, and it does compile in ModelSim (2020.1).


### Actual Behavior

Yosys reports an error when loading this module.
```
$ yosys -p "read_verilog esc.v"

 /----------------------------------------------------------------------------\

 Yosys 0.33+53 (git sha1 e2b613355, clang 14.0.0-1ubuntu1.1 -fPIC -Os)


-- Running command `read_verilog esc.v' --

1. Executing Verilog-2005 frontend: esc.v
Parsing Verilog input from `esc.v' to AST representation.
Generating RTLIL representation for module `\esc'.
esc.v:1: ERROR: Incompatible re-declaration of wire \a[1].
```
